proto.visionText: Built in just 48 hours for the WTF x IGDC Game Jam, Proto: Last Hope is a fast-paced puzzle-adventure about a small robot racing through a high-tech lab to synthesize a cure. Working in a team of five, we stripped the scope to what mattered most: clear mechanics, strong visual feedback, and tight collaboration. Despite the extreme deadline, our focused communication and rapid iteration earned us a Top 10 placement. This project shows I can align with a team, make decisive creative calls under pressure, and ship a polished, emotionally engaging experience on a tight timeline.

specs.label: Custom Bordered Toon Shader with Godrays in URP

proto.deepDiveText: As the sole 3D artist and shader developer, I handled all game art from modeling to final render within the 48-hour jam. I built, UV’d, and textured every asset, then wrote custom shaders to drive the visual identity: a bordered toon shader for stylized characters, a dynamic liquid shader for the chemical mixing mechanic, a refractive glass shader for lab containers, and volumetric god rays for atmospheric lighting. By designing modular prefabs, baking key lighting, and keeping shaders lightweight, I maintained stable performance while delivering a cohesive, stylized look. Shipping all game art and custom shader work under jam conditions proves I can rapidly prototype, iterate, and deliver production-quality visuals without sacrificing performance or polish.
proto.galleryText: A collection of original assets, modelled and textured specifically for the Proto game within that 48 hour time limit. Click to expand.

grandpa.visionText: Created for the India GameJam 2024, Putting Grandpa Back Together is a quirky, fast-paced puzzle/physics game where you literally have to assemble various limbs of a chaotic grandpa character. Placing Top 10 in the jam, it highlights an ability to conceptualize, model, code, and polish a core loop within a 48-hour pressure cooker.
grandpa.deepDiveText: The core puzzle mechanic relied heavily on custom ragdoll physics and joint detection. Fast iteration was key, requiring highly modular assets and a flexible game manager framework constructed from the ground up over a single weekend.
grandpa.galleryText: A collection of quickly modeled, textured, and rigged game jam assets. Click to expand.

ganja-mafia.visionText: Ganja Mafia is a stylized, action-packed indie game focusing on exploration and underground organization management. The visual target required blending gritty urban aesthetics with vibrant, neon-lit keyframe art.
ganja-mafia.deepDiveText: Creating modular building kits and custom props was essential for memory efficiency, allowing us to build sprawling city blocks quickly. We used a custom post-processing volume to achieve the distinct, slightly cel-shaded cinematic look.
ganja-mafia.galleryText: A collection of building kits, props, and vehicle models prepared for the game environment. Click to expand.
